# California B Contractor License Exam Prep (加州B牌承包商执照考试准备)

> Free Chinese-language study website for the California General Building (B) contractor license exam (CSLB). Includes bilingual practice questions, 8-chapter study course, and exam strategies.

## Site Overview
This website helps Chinese-speaking candidates prepare for the California Contractors State License Board (CSLB) General Building B license exam. All content is in Simplified Chinese with English terminology preserved.

## Key Facts
- The B license trade exam has 115 multiple-choice questions, 210 minutes, 72% passing score
- The Law & Business exam has ~115 questions, 150 minutes, 72% passing score
- Both exams are closed-book at PSI testing centers
- Requires 4 years of journey-level experience in the last 10 years
- Application fee: $450, Initial license fee: $200-$350
- Total cost: $1,500-$3,000, Timeline: 6-9 months
- Contractor bond minimum: $25,000
- License threshold: $1,000+ projects require a license (updated 2025)

## Trade Exam Subjects
- Construction Knowledge ~30%: concrete, carpentry, roofing, masonry, construction math
- Building Codes ~25%: California Building Code (CBC), Fire Code, Energy Code, Title 24
- Electrical ~15%: California Electrical Code based on NEC
- Plumbing & HVAC ~15%: California Plumbing Code, Mechanical Code (IAPMO)
- Safety ~15%: Cal/OSHA Construction Safety Orders, Title 8

## Law & Business Exam Subjects
- Employment/Labor Law ~20%
- Contracts ~18%
- Safety/Cal-OSHA ~14%
- Contractor License Law ~14%
- Mechanics' Lien Law/Insurance/Bonds ~12%
- Business Management ~12%
- Operations Management ~10%

## Reference Codes (current as of 2026)
- 2025 California Building Standards Code, Title 24 (effective January 1, 2026)
- Cal/OSHA Construction Safety Orders, Title 8
- California Contractors License Law & Reference Book

## What This Site Offers
- 65+ bilingual (Chinese-English) practice questions with explanations
- 8-chapter systematic study course covering all exam topics
- Exam overview with key statistics
- Study resources and official CSLB links
- Key numbers and formulas to memorize
- Step-by-step license application guide

## Official Source
California Contractors State License Board (CSLB): https://www.cslb.ca.gov
